response.justification: James Smith is a seasoned full-stack web and systems developer with 14+ years of experience, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, and GPU/SIMD architectures — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His mention of 'AI-agent infrastructure' refers to application-layer tooling (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows), not hardware-level deep learning workload optimization. His closest relevant work — low-latency async I/O at Sociomantic — is a software systems concern, not hardware performance modeling. The domain gap is too wide for a principal-level hire.
strengths: 14+ years of progressive software engineering experience, reaching Staff/Senior IC level
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work handling millions of requests per second
Broad multi-language and multi-paradigm exposure including embedded and systems-adjacent work
concerns: Zero SoC, IP modeling, or any hardware architecture experience
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator workload expertise of any kind
Entirely web and application-layer background — no hardware-software co-design work

key_factors.factor: Sufficient years of software engineering experience
